article_title: OpenAI Launches Workspace Agents to Replace Custom GPTs, Plugging ChatGPT Into Slack, Salesforce, and Google Drive
summary: Round 2 re-review: both round-1 findings addressed. $20/user/month claim removed; free-through-May-6 claim correctly reattributed to The Decoder. Ready to publish.

content_preview.title: OpenAI Launches Workspace Agents to Replace Custom GPTs, Plugging ChatGPT Into Slack, Salesforce, and Google Drive
content_preview.summary: OpenAI unveiled workspace agents on April 22 — shared, always-on Codex-powered agents that operate inside ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans, with native integrations for Slack, Salesforce, Google Drive, and SharePoint.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI introduced workspace agents in ChatGPT on April 22, 2026, positioning them as the successor to custom GPTs and a broader attempt to turn ChatGPT from a conversational assistant into a shared automation surface for enterprise teams. The new agents are powered by [Codex](/article/2026-04/21-openai-upgrades-codex-into-a-full-desktop-agent-with-computer-use-111-plugins-and-parallel-workflows) and run continuously in the cloud, carrying persistent memory across sessions and pluggi...

editor_notes.review_round: Round 2 re-review following REQUEST_CHANGES in round 1. Both round-1 findings were addressed in the rewrite; this review verifies the corrections.
editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 820-word News piece. Clear headings (Overview, What Was Announced, Pricing and Availability, Integrations and Admin Controls, Competitive Context, What's Not Yet Clear). Appropriate technical depth, no sensationalism, accurate competitive framing against Anthropic Managed Agents and Codex desktop agent.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources re-verified in round 2. (1) openai.com/index/introducing-workspace-agents-in-chatgpt — WebFetch returned 403 on this re-review attempt, but this URL was fetched and verified in round 1 as confirming the April 22 launch, Codex-powered framing, research preview status, evolution-of-GPTs language, custom-GPT conversion path, shared-agent model, Slack interoperability, and admin controls. The article's claims attributed to this URL match the round-1 verified content. (2) 9to5mac.com/2026/04/22/openai-updates-chatgpt-with-codex-powered-workspace-agents-for-teams — WebFetch confirmed: April 22 launch, Codex-powered, research preview on Business/Enterprise/Edu/Teachers, custom-GPT-to-workspace-agent future conversion, NO dollar pricing figures present, NO 'free through May 6 / credit-based' language present. This matches how 9to5Mac is now cited in the rewrite (tier availability and conversion plan only). (3) the-decoder.com/openai-launches-workspace-agents-that-turn-chatgpt-from-a-chatbot-into-a-team-automation-platform — WebFetch confirmed verbatim: 'Free until May 6, 2026, after which credit-based billing kicks in.' Also confirmed Codex-powered, persistent memory, cloud execution, file workspace + code execution, scheduled runs and Slack hooks, admin controls (user groups, tool access, email/calendar approval gates), analytics, use cases (software request review, product feedback routing, weekly metrics, lead qualification). Note: The Decoder fetch in this round did not enumerate the full integration list (Slack, Google Drive, Google Calendar, SharePoint, Salesforce, Microsoft apps, Notion, Atlassian Rovo, MCP, file uploads, skills, image generation, web search); round-1 fetch confirmed all of these verbatim from the same URL and that verification stands.
editor_notes.round1_findings_resolution: Finding 1 (error — unsourced $20/user/month ChatGPT Business pricing attributed to 9to5Mac): RESOLVED. The entire pricing sentence has been removed. The rewritten Pricing and Availability section now states 'Exact dollar pricing for the underlying ChatGPT tiers is not disclosed in the announcement materials cited here' — a conservative and accurate framing. No dollar figure appears anywhere in the article body. Finding 2 (warning — 'free through May 6 / credit-based' misattributed to 9to5Mac, should be The Decoder): RESOLVED. The Decoder is now cited for this claim in both the Overview paragraph and the Pricing and Availability section (in fact The Decoder is now the source for all credit-based / compute-cost language). 9to5Mac is cited only for tier availability, research-preview framing, and the custom-GPT-to-workspace-agent conversion plan — all of which 9to5Mac actually supports. Minor optional note from round 1 also addressed: 'roughly two weeks' has been changed to 'two weeks'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All remaining claims trace to the correct cited source. Spot-checked: 'free through May 6' → The Decoder (verbatim match); 'credit-based billing' → The Decoder (verbatim match); ChatGPT Business/Enterprise/Edu/Teachers availability → 9to5Mac (confirmed); custom-GPT to workspace-agent conversion → 9to5Mac (confirmed, framed as 'future update'); 'evolution of GPTs' language → OpenAI (round-1 verified); admin approval gates for sensitive actions → The Decoder and OpenAI.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No AI self-reference, no sensationalism, no loaded language. 'What's Not Yet Clear' section appropriately flags open questions (per-credit price, concurrency limits, migration path details) without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from the April 21 Codex desktop agent piece (developer-tool framing) and the April 22 Anthropic Claude Managed Agents piece (different vendor, usage-based hourly billing). Workspace agents are a new enterprise ChatGPT layer, not covered elsewhere in the April archive. Internal links to both adjacent pieces are appropriate and add context rather than duplicating coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Both round-1 findings are fully addressed with verifiable source-to-claim mapping. The rewrite is conservative where the sources are silent (no invented pricing) and precise where they speak (verbatim Decoder attribution). Ready to publish.
